sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: 1. FC Union Berlin are struggling, having failed to win in their last 7 matches, with defensive frailties evident as they've conceded in their last 8 games. In contrast, Bayer 04 Leverkusen are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 7 matches across all competitions, showcasing attacking prowess and consistency. Leverkusen's recent performances indicate they are likely to dominate possession and create more scoring opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Leverkusen hold a significant edge in recent encounters, remaining unbeaten against Union Berlin in their last 10 meetings, including a convincing win in the most recent matchup this season. Historical data suggests Leverkusen often take the initiative early, having scored first in 4 of the last 5 head-to-head clashes. Union Berlin will need to overcome this trend to stand a chance at home.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen, sitting 6th in the Bundesliga with 39 points, have shown superior attacking output compared to Union Berlin, who are 10th with 25 points and rank low in possession and pass accuracy. Union Berlin's strength lies in creating chances from crosses, ranking high in accurate crosses, but their conversion rate remains a concern. Leverkusen’s balanced attack and defensive solidity make them the favorites according to season-long metrics.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: For Union Berlin, players like Ilyas Ansah will be crucial in breaking through Leverkusen’s defense, while their high number of penalty area entries could pose a threat if converted. Leverkusen’s in-form players like Patrik Schick and Aleix García are expected to exploit Union’s defensive vulnerabilities, especially with their ability to score and create in critical moments. Leverkusen’s tactical discipline and pressing could overwhelm Union’s midfield.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, 1. FC Union Berlin holds a narrow lead over Bayer 04 Leverkusen with a score of 1-0, thanks to a well-taken goal by Rani Khedira in the 28th minute. Union Berlin has shown resilience and capitalized on their chance, while Leverkusen has struggled to convert their possession into meaningful opportunities. The game has been competitive, with both teams having their moments, but Union's defensive structure and clinical finishing have given them the edge so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Union Berlin has been effective in their approach, creating a significant opportunity and converting it into a goal. Their defensive organization has been key, limiting Leverkusen to minimal clear chances despite the away side dominating possession. Leverkusen, on the other hand, has seen more of the ball but failed to penetrate Union's defense effectively. Their inability to register a shot on target in the first half highlights their struggles in the final third, and they will need to sharpen their attack to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: Rani Khedira has been pivotal for Union Berlin, not only scoring the opener but also contributing to the midfield battle. Derrick Köhn has also stood out with his involvement in creating opportunities from the flank. For Leverkusen, Robert Andrich has tried to drive the team forward from midfield, while Alejandro Grimaldo has shown intent with his forward runs, though the final product has been lacking. Both teams will look to their key players to inspire a shift in momentum after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Union Berlin start with strong attacking intent, pushing forward with purpose in the opening minutes. Leverkusen responded with a spell of pressure, attempting to assert control, but Union regained momentum with sustained attacks leading up to their goal. After conceding, Leverkusen had periods of dominance, especially towards the end of the half, as they pressed for an equalizer. However, Union's defense held firm during these phases, preventing any real breakthroughs.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Disciplinary concerns
halftimeSections.text: Union Berlin has picked up three yellow cards in the first half, which could impact their approach in the second half as they balance aggression with caution. Players like Janik Haberer, Andrej Ilić, and Stanley N'Soki will need to tread carefully to avoid further bookings or potential dismissals. Leverkusen, meanwhile, has kept a clean disciplinary record, giving them more freedom to press high and challenge Union without immediate fear of sanctions.
